title: podman-auto-update.service fails by using both tag and digest references
body:
### Issue Description

Using `podman-auto-update.service`
Some images just use the `latest` tag.
 So to update them I need to change its hash so podman can download a new version.
 (Renovate bot automates the updating of my yaml files)
 
 The autoupdater just errors out which lets services fail using it as an dependency.  
Also it prevents the image pruning I think.

It would be better to just emit a warning and maybe implement this missing feature.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Using `podman-auto-update.service` with tags just having the `latest` tag and a changing hash.

### Describe the results you received

```
$ systemctl --user status podman-auto-update.service --output cat
× podman-auto-update.service - Podman auto-update service
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman-auto-update.service; disabled; preset: enabled)
    Drop-In: /home/servername/.config/systemd/user/service.d
             └─toplevel-override.conf
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Sun 2024-01-07 13:47:43 CET; 22min ago
TriggeredBy: ● podman-auto-update.timer
       Docs: man:podman-auto-update(1)
   Main PID: 10266 (code=exited, status=125)
        CPU: 46ms

Error: 5 errors occurred:
        * checking image updates for container 113019fb2f2ed9e9a0501e50fd250b8536e862e16939f397f73cdd11f3392776: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
        * checking image updates for container 1a387ed894f9e92f41e635bda421bc6d4cd7740464f5944aed5bea6ff2d2f1fe: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
        * checking image updates for container 3046efefd57d96d77730c248c3561c63e93bc9efc505405336f4aa4640636202: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
        * checking image updates for container 4a194eead5ae238f1ae17141aafef2192f65ef0e7d97cdc81dcf502a89ec07ef: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
        * checking image updates for container d547a1300a6b5895ef7569efd3813688e354373a31a504bc224327e9b9a75fe2: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
podman-auto-update.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
podman-auto-update.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Failed to start Podman auto-update service.
```

### Describe the results you expected

Just a warning.

### podman info output


### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
The error seems like a bug because podman pull and run work since https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10486.

That said there is really no point in using auto-update when you use the digest, even if it would work correctly auto-update would never find a new image with that so it would never update anything. podman auto-update only looks at the image reference that was used to create the container, it will never check the systemd unit so if renovate updates the digest in file it will have no effect for auto-update and you manually have to restart your service anyway.

But yeah if you some images with a tag and some by digest it should clearly not error out. I think it is best to just ignore images with digest. @vrothberg WDYT?

> But yeah if you some images with a tag and some by digest it should clearly not error out. I think it is best to just ignore images with digest. @vrothberg WDYT?

I think failing is the right thing to do.  If a user configured a container for auto updates with a digested reference, it can't work as you explained above.  I prefer throwing an error over ignoring/suppressing as it may otherwise give the illusion of auto updates to work.

I'm forced to use some images with the version tag and some image with the `latest` tag, because there only the latest tag gets updates (so only the digest changes).  

In the background the tags and the digest for the `latest` in my kube.yaml files are updated by the renovate bot.  

How should i handle it?
I get an error message every day.
Do not set the auto update label on containers where you use an image with digest I think.

You mean to set the auto update label to deactivate  it?  
Because i didn't set it here

https://github.com/MartinX3-AdministrativeDevelopment/ServerContainerTemplate/blob/master/container%2Fservices%2Fldap%2Fldap-pod.yaml

https://github.com/MartinX3-AdministrativeDevelopment/ServerContainerTemplate/blob/ae572e6cfb885c4e8c0bbe93c43e6121ec38455c/container/services/ldap/ldap-pod.yaml#L6

This should not be set unless you want the auto update with podman-auto-update.service

Argh i feel stupid now.  
Thank you!  

I think the error message should hint to remove the flag.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> > But yeah if you some images with a tag and some by digest it should clearly not error out. I think it is best to just ignore images with digest. @vrothberg WDYT?
> 
> I think failing is the right thing to do. If a user configured a container for auto updates with a digested reference, it can't work as you explained above. I prefer throwing an error over ignoring/suppressing as it may otherwise give the illusion of auto updates to work.

I am fine with failing but the current error makes no sense in the end the user was able to create such contianer with such image definition in the first place. I guess the error should say `auto-update with digest reference is not supported` instead.
